query: AELTC Wimbledon Park expansion 2026 39 courts £200m planning permission GLA former golf course latest update
a_issues: Content A is somewhat mixed on whether the expansion is 38 or 39 new courts. The London City Hall result says 38 additional grass courts plus a new show court, while other results describe 39 new courts total. This is reconcilable but could confuse a model. One BBC result snippet refers specifically to a later appeal-court challenge without much detail, so by itself it is incomplete. Otherwise the key planning-permission and legal-update facts are accurate and sourced to reliable outlets and official pages.
b_issues: Content B also mixes 38 and 39 courts, and several results are thin or lower-value for answering the latest update. The top result is a YouTube protest clip rather than a strong primary or mainstream text source. It lacks the key official planning-permission source from the GLA/City Hall. Some snippets are incomplete or vague, e.g. Forbes/ESPN. While The Athletic and Al Jazeera mention the High Court ruling, B provides weaker grounding on the planning-permission history and latest procedural status.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it includes the crucial official source showing that the GLA/Deputy Mayor approved planning permission in 2024 for the former Wimbledon Park golf course redevelopment, with 38 additional grass courts and a new show court. It also includes reliable secondary sources (BBC, Guardian, Straits Times) that place this in the latest 2026 legal context: Save Wimbledon Park’s challenge was dismissed and the High Court ruled the land was not subject to the claimed restrictions. That is very close to the Reuters anchor, which says the 2026 High Court rejected campaigners’ attempt to block the project. B has some correct latest-update material, especially the March 2026 court ruling, but it is less complete and less reliable overall because it lacks the official GLA approval result and leans on a YouTube protest video and partial snippets. Therefore A gives an LLM a stronger factual basis to answer the query correctly.
